meta.title: ScrollyMaker Demo Lesson
meta.description: A three-step chart-driven explainer that demonstrates reusable components, CMS-style block editing, and state transitions.

chart1.title: Learner confidence across lesson phases
chart1.description: Illustrative chart showing increasing learner confidence as instructional supports are layered in.

overview.annotation: The baseline view introduces the pattern before emphasizing any one step.

highlight.annotation: Guided scroll steps raise confidence when the story actively focuses attention.

zoom.annotation: Giving learners a moment to directly explore the content deepens engagement further.

summaryText.content: State-driven components can be reused across steps without recreating the DOM each time.

steps.title: Start with an overview state
steps.body: The same chart component appears throughout the story. Each step simply requests a new named state instead of rebuilding the chart.

blocks.label: Scene introduction
blocks.content: Use the left-hand canvas to place and review blocks directly in the story flow. Then use the inspector to refine content and settings.

blocks.label: Authoring pattern
blocks.content: This mirrors the feel of familiar CMS builders: add blocks where you are composing, configure them in a dedicated side panel.

steps.title: Guide attention with scroll-triggered updates
steps.body: As the step activates, the runtime calls `update(state)` on the same chart instance. This creates a focused instructional beat without changing layout structure.

blocks.label: Instructional framing
blocks.content: Keep narrative copy in stacked blocks so authors can mix text, callouts, image placeholders, and later embeds within the same scene.

blocks.label: Media placeholder
blocks.caption: A placeholder image block demonstrates how media can appear inside the WYSIWYG stack while the chart remains the reusable media component.

steps.title: End with a deeper exploration state
steps.body: The final step transitions to a zoomed interpretation of the data and reinforces how scroll guidance can hand off to richer interaction patterns later.

blocks.label: Deeper exploration
blocks.content: Future versions can let these blocks represent 3D scenes, quizzes, audio, embeds, or data-rich interactives while keeping the authoring pattern consistent.

blocks.label: Embed placeholder
blocks.caption: Use this kind of block later for maps, custom iframes, simulations, or other external interactive media.
